name: US Rust Belt
climate: Humid continental; cold snowy winters, warm humid summers; lake-effect snow near Great Lakes

neighbors.name: US Corn Belt
neighbors.relevance: Adjacent agricultural region; farm equipment demand, ethanol plants, and food processing create tight economic links

neighbors.name: USMCA
neighbors.relevance: Rust Belt sits within USMCA territory; US-Canada auto supply chains cross the Great Lakes border daily

neighbors.name: Silicon Valley
neighbors.relevance: Tech-driven automation and EV software reshape Rust Belt manufacturing; venture capital flows into industrial IoT

neighbors.name: European Union
neighbors.relevance: EU automakers (VW, BMW, Stellantis) operate Rust Belt plants; steel tariffs affect transatlantic trade

neighbors.name: RCEP
neighbors.relevance: Asian steel and auto parts imports compete with Rust Belt output; China, Japan, South Korea are key rivals

links.description: Rust Belt automotive and steel industries are deeply integrated into US-Canada-Mexico supply chains
Great Lakes corridor processes Corn Belt agricultural output; ethanol and food manufacturing overlap
EV transition depends on Silicon Valley battery and software tech; reshoring of chip manufacturing
European steel and auto imports compete directly; tariff and anti-dumping disputes are recurring
Chinese steel overcapacity and Asian auto parts undercut Rust Belt producers; tariff protection is key
G7 steel and aluminum trade agreements directly affect Rust Belt production levels and employment
Great Lakes-St. Lawrence Seaway and Gulf Coast ports connect Rust Belt exports to global markets via Panama
